children.heading: Competitive grants, contracts, and cooperative agreements authorized
children.label: a Competitive grants, contracts, and cooperative agreements authorized

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: From amounts appropriated under , the Secretary may award grants, contracts, and cooperative agreements, on a competitive basis, to institutions of higher education to enable the institutions to carry out the activities under subsection (b).section 1140e of this title

children.heading: Awards for professional development and technical assistance
children.label: 2 Awards for professional development and technical assistance
children.text: Not less than two grants, contracts, cooperative agreements, or a combination of such awards shall be awarded to institutions of higher education that provide professional development and technical assistance in order for students with learning disabilities to receive a quality postsecondary education.

children.heading: Duration
children.label: 1 Duration
children.text: A grant, contract, or cooperative agreement under this subpart shall be awarded for a period of three years.

children.heading: Teaching methods and strategies
children.label: A Teaching methods and strategies
children.text: The development of innovative, effective, and efficient teaching methods and strategies, consistent with the principles of universal design for learning, to provide postsecondary faculty, staff, and administrators with the skills and supports necessary to teach and meet the academic and programmatic needs of students with disabilities, in order to improve the retention of such students in, and the completion by such students of, postsecondary education. Such methods and strategies may include in-service training, professional development, customized and general technical assistance, workshops, summer institutes, distance learning, and training in the use of assistive and educational technology.

children.heading: Effective transition practices
children.label: B Effective transition practices
children.text: The development of innovative and effective teaching methods and strategies to provide postsecondary faculty, staff, and administrators with the skill and supports necessary to ensure the successful and smooth transition of students with disabilities from secondary school to postsecondary education.

children.heading: Synthesizing research and information
children.label: C Synthesizing research and information
children.text: The synthesis of research and other information related to the provision of postsecondary educational services to students with disabilities, including data on the impact of a postsecondary education on subsequent employment of students with disabilities. Such research, information, and data shall be made publicly available and accessible.

children.heading: Distance learning
children.label: D Distance learning
children.text: The development of innovative and effective teaching methods and strategies to provide postsecondary faculty, staff, and administrators with the ability to provide accessible distance education programs or classes that would enhance the access of students with disabilities to postsecondary education, including the use of accessible curricula and electronic communication for instruction and advising.

children.label: I
children.text: encourage interest and participation in such fields, among students with disabilities and other students;

children.label: II
children.text: enhance awareness and understanding of such fields among students with disabilities and other students;

children.label: III
children.text: provide educational opportunities in such fields for students with disabilities and other students;

children.label: IV
children.text: teach practical skills related to such fields to students with disabilities and other students; and

children.label: V
children.text: offer work-based opportunities in such fields to students with disabilities and other students.

children.label: I
children.text: credit-bearing postsecondary-level coursework; and

children.label: II
children.text: career and educational counseling.

children.heading: Professional development and training sessions
children.label: F Professional development and training sessions
children.text: The conduct of professional development and training sessions for postsecondary faculty, staff, and administrators from other institutions of higher education to enable such individuals to meet the educational needs of students with disabilities.

children.heading: Accessibility of education
children.label: G Accessibility of education
children.text: Making postsecondary education more accessible to students with disabilities through curriculum development, consistent with the principles of universal design for learning.

children.heading: Mandatory evaluation and dissemination
children.label: 3 Mandatory evaluation and dissemination
children.text: An institution of higher education awarded a grant, contract, or cooperative agreement under this subpart shall evaluate and disseminate to other institutions of higher education, the information obtained through the activities described in subparagraphs (A) through (G) of paragraph (2).

children.heading: Geographic distribution
children.label: 1 Geographic distribution
children.text: Providing an equitable geographic distribution of such awards.

children.heading: Rural and urban areas
children.label: 2 Rural and urban areas
children.text: Distributing such awards to urban and rural areas.

children.heading: Range and type of institution
children.label: 3 Range and type of institution
children.text: Ensuring that the activities to be assisted are developed for a range of types and sizes of institutions of higher education.

children.heading: Prior experience or exceptional programs
children.label: 4 Prior experience or exceptional programs
children.text: Distributing the awards to institutions of higher education with demonstrated prior experience in, or exceptional programs for, meeting the postsecondary educational needs of students with disabilities.

children.heading: Initial report
children.label: 1 Initial report
children.text: Not later than one year after , the Secretary shall prepare and submit to the authorizing committees, and make available to the public, a report on all demonstration projects awarded grants under this part for any of fiscal years 1999 through 2008, including a review of the activities and program performance of such demonstration projects based on existing information as of the date of the report.August 14, 2008

children.label: A
children.text: reviews the activities and program performance of the demonstration projects authorized under this subpart; and

children.label: B
children.text: provides guidance and recommendations on how effective projects can be replicated.
